+"""Simple CLI entry-point for running the HN monitor."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import asyncio
+import logging
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from .db.store import Store
+from .hn.client import HNClient
+from .hn.monitor import HNMonitor
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    logging.basicConfig(
+        level=logging.INFO,
+        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s: %(message)s",
+    )
+    logger = logging.getLogger("company_sourcing")
+
+    db_path = Path("data/sourcing.db")
+    fetch_limit = 60
+
+    # Simple arg handling
+    for arg in sys.argv[1:]:
+        if arg.startswith("--db="):
+            db_path = Path(arg.split("=", 1)[1])
+        elif arg.startswith("--limit="):
+            fetch_limit = int(arg.split("=", 1)[1])
+
+    logger.info("Starting HN monitor (db=%s, limit=%d)", db_path, fetch_limit)
+    store = Store(db_path)
+    client = HNClient()
+
+    async def _run() -> None:
+        monitor = HNMonitor(client=client, store=store)
+        result = await monitor.run(fetch_limit=fetch_limit)
+        print(f"\nDone! Fetched {result.stories_fetched} stories.")
+        print(f"Signals found: {result.signals_found}")
+        print(f"New signals stored: {result.new_signals_stored}")
+        print(f"New companies discovered: {result.new_companies}")
+        if result.errors:
+            print(f"Errors: {len(result.errors)}")
+            for err in result.errors:
+                print(f"  - {err}")
+        await client.close()
+        store.close()
+
+    asyncio.run(_run())
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+"""Database models using Pydantic for validation and SQLite for storage."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import enum
+from datetime import datetime, timezone
+
+from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
+
+
+class Source(str, enum.Enum):
+    """Where we found the signal."""
+
+    HACKERNEWS = "hackernews"
+    TWITTER = "twitter"
+    GOOGLE_NEWS = "google_news"
+
+
+class SignalType(str, enum.Enum):
+    """What kind of signal this is."""
+
+    SHOW_HN = "show_hn"
+    PRODUCT_LAUNCH = "product_launch"
+    FUNDING = "funding"
+    HIRING = "hiring"
+    GENERAL_MENTION = "general_mention"
+
+
+class Company(BaseModel):
+    """An extracted company entity."""
+
+    id: int | None = None
+    name: str
+    description: str = ""
+    url: str = ""
+    first_seen_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
+
+
+class Signal(BaseModel):
+    """A signal about a company from a source."""
+
+    id: int | None = None
+    company_name: str
+    signal_type: SignalType
+    source: Source
+    source_id: str  # e.g. HN story ID
+    source_url: str = ""
+    title: str = ""
+    content: str = ""
+    score: int = 0  # HN points, tweet likes, etc.
+    comments_count: int = 0
+    discovered_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
+
+
+class HNItem(BaseModel):
+    """A raw HackerNews item from the API."""
+
+    id: int
+    type: str = ""
+    title: str = ""
+    text: str = ""
+    url: str = ""
+    by: str = ""
+    score: int = 0
+    descendants: int = 0  # comment count
+    time: int = 0

@@ -0,0 +1,137 @@
+"""SQLite storage layer for companies and signals."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import sqlite3
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from .models import Company, Signal
+
+DEFAULT_DB_PATH = Path("data/sourcing.db")
+
+SCHEMA = """\
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS companies (
+    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
+    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
+    description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    first_seen_at TEXT NOT NULL
+);
+
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS signals (
+    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
+    company_name TEXT NOT NULL,
+    signal_type TEXT NOT NULL,
+    source TEXT NOT NULL,
+    source_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    source_url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    content TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
+    comments_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
+    discovered_at TEXT NOT NULL,
+    UNIQUE(source, source_id, company_name)
+);
+
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_company ON signals(company_name);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_source ON signals(source);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON signals(signal_type);
+"""
+
+
+class Store:
+    """Thin wrapper around SQLite for persisting companies and signals."""
+
+    def __init__(self, db_path: Path = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
+        self.db_path = db_path
+        self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        self._conn = sqlite3.connect(str(self.db_path))
+        self._conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
+        self._conn.executescript(SCHEMA)
+
+    # -- Companies ----------------------------------------------------------
+
+    def upsert_company(self, company: Company) -> Company:
+        """Insert or ignore a company (keyed by name)."""
+        cur = self._conn.execute(
+            """INSERT INTO companies (name, description, url, first_seen_at)
+               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
+               ON CONFLICT(name) DO UPDATE SET
+                   description = CASE WHEN excluded.description != '' THEN excluded.description ELSE companies.description END,
+                   url = CASE WHEN excluded.url != '' THEN excluded.url ELSE companies.url END
+            """,
+            (
+                company.name,
+                company.description,
+                company.url,
+                company.first_seen_at.isoformat(),
+            ),
+        )
+        self._conn.commit()
+        company.id = cur.lastrowid
+        return company
+
+    def get_company(self, name: str) -> Company | None:
+        row = self._conn.execute(
+            "SELECT * FROM companies WHERE name = ?", (name,)
+        ).fetchone()
+        if row is None:
+            return None
+        return Company(**dict(row))
+
+    def list_companies(self, limit: int = 100) -> list[Company]:
+        rows = self._conn.execute(
+            "SELECT * FROM companies ORDER BY first_seen_at DESC LIMIT ?", (limit,)
+        ).fetchall()
+        return [Company(**dict(r)) for r in rows]
+
+    # -- Signals ------------------------------------------------------------
+
+    def insert_signal(self, signal: Signal) -> Signal | None:
+        """Insert a signal. Returns None if duplicate (source+source_id+company)."""
+        try:
+            cur = self._conn.execute(
+                """INSERT INTO signals
+                   (company_name, signal_type, source, source_id, source_url,
+                    title, content, score, comments_count, discovered_at)
+                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
+                (
+                    signal.company_name,
+                    signal.signal_type,
+                    signal.source,
+                    signal.source_id,
+                    signal.source_url,
+                    signal.title,
+                    signal.content,
+                    signal.score,
+                    signal.comments_count,
+                    signal.discovered_at.isoformat(),
+                ),
+            )
+            self._conn.commit()
+            signal.id = cur.lastrowid
+            return signal
+        except sqlite3.IntegrityError:
+            return None
+
+    def list_signals(
+        self,
+        company_name: str | None = None,
+        source: str | None = None,
+        limit: int = 100,
+    ) -> list[Signal]:
+        query = "SELECT * FROM signals WHERE 1=1"
+        params: list[object] = []
+        if company_name:
+            query += " AND company_name = ?"
+            params.append(company_name)
+        if source:
+            query += " AND source = ?"
+            params.append(source)
+        query += " ORDER BY discovered_at DESC LIMIT ?"
+        params.append(limit)
+        rows = self._conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()
+        return [Signal(**dict(r)) for r in rows]
+
+    def close(self) -> None:
+        self._conn.close()

@@ -0,0 +1,175 @@
+"""Extract company mentions and classify signals from HN posts.
+
+Strategy:
+  1. Pattern-match on well-known HN title formats (Show HN, Launch HN, etc.)
+  2. Look for funding/launch keywords in title and body text
+  3. Extract company name from the title using heuristics
+  4. Classify the signal type (show_hn, funding, product_launch, etc.)
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import re
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+
+from ..db.models import HNItem, SignalType
+
+
+@dataclass
+class ExtractionResult:
+    """Result of extracting a company signal from an HN item."""
+
+    company_name: str
+    signal_type: SignalType
+    description: str
+
+
+# Patterns ordered by specificity
+SHOW_HN_RE = re.compile(r"^Show HN:\s*(.+)", re.IGNORECASE)
+LAUNCH_HN_RE = re.compile(r"^Launch HN:\s*(.+)", re.IGNORECASE)
+
+# Funding patterns: "Company raises $X", "Company announces Series A", etc.
+FUNDING_RE = re.compile(
+    r"(.+?)\s+(?:raises?|raised|secures?|secured|closes?|closed|announces?|announced)"
+    r"\s+(?:\$[\d.]+[BMK]?\s+)?(?:(?:seed|series\s+[a-z]|funding|round|investment))",
+    re.IGNORECASE,
+)
+
+# "Company launches X", "We built X", "Introducing X"
+LAUNCH_KEYWORDS_RE = re.compile(
+    r"(.+?)\s+(?:launch(?:es|ed)?|releas(?:es|ed|ing)|introducing|we\s+built|just\s+shipped)",
+    re.IGNORECASE,
+)
+
+# YC batch pattern: "Company (YC S24)"
+YC_BATCH_RE = re.compile(r"(.+?)\s*\(YC\s+[A-Z]\d{2}\)", re.IGNORECASE)
+
+# Common title fluff to strip when extracting company names
+FLUFF_RE = re.compile(
+    r"\s*[-–—:|]\s*(an?\s+|the\s+)?(open[- ]source\s+)?.*$",
+    re.IGNORECASE,
+)
+
+# Words that are almost certainly not company names
+STOP_WORDS = frozenset(
+    {
+        "how",
+        "why",
+        "what",
+        "when",
+        "where",
+        "who",
+        "ask",
+        "tell",
+        "the",
+        "a",
+        "an",
+        "is",
+        "are",
+        "was",
+        "i",
+        "we",
+        "my",
+        "our",
+    }
+)
+
+
+def _clean_company_name(raw: str) -> str:
+    """Best-effort cleanup of a raw company name string."""
+    # Remove markdown links
+    name = re.sub(r"\[([^\]]+)\]\([^)]+\)", r"\1", raw)
+    # Remove HTML tags
+    name = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", name)
+    # Take text before common separators that introduce a description
+    name = FLUFF_RE.sub("", name)
+    name = name.strip().strip(".,;:!?\"'")
+
+    # If what's left is very long, take the first segment before a dash/colon
+    if len(name) > 60:
+        parts = re.split(r"\s*[-–—:]\s*", name, maxsplit=1)
+        name = parts[0].strip()
+
+    return name
+
+
+def _looks_like_company(name: str) -> bool:
+    """Quick heuristic to filter out obvious non-company extractions."""
+    if not name or len(name) < 2:
+        return False
+    first_word = name.split()[0].lower()
+    if first_word in STOP_WORDS:
+        return False
+    # Reject if it's all lowercase and reads like a sentence
+    if len(name.split()) > 6:
+        return False
+    return True
+
+
+def extract_from_hn_item(item: HNItem) -> ExtractionResult | None:
+    """Try to extract a company signal from an HN item.
+
+    Returns None if no company signal is detected.
+    """
+    title = item.title.strip()
+    if not title:
+        return None
+
+    # --- Show HN ---
+    m = SHOW_HN_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        raw = m.group(1)
+        name = _clean_company_name(raw)
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.SHOW_HN,
+                description=raw.strip(),
+            )
+
+    # --- Launch HN ---
+    m = LAUNCH_HN_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        raw = m.group(1)
+        name = _clean_company_name(raw)
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.PRODUCT_LAUNCH,
+                description=raw.strip(),
+            )
+
+    # --- YC batch mentions ---
+    m = YC_BATCH_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        name = _clean_company_name(m.group(1))
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.FUNDING,
+                description=title,
+            )
+
+    # --- Funding announcements ---
+    m = FUNDING_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        name = _clean_company_name(m.group(1))
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.FUNDING,
+                description=title,
+            )
+
+    # --- Product launches / "we built" ---
+    m = LAUNCH_KEYWORDS_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        name = _clean_company_name(m.group(1))
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.PRODUCT_LAUNCH,
+                description=title,
+            )
+
+    return None

+"""Async client for the HackerNews Firebase API.
+
+Docs: https://github.com/HackerNews/API
+Base URL: https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import logging
+from typing import Any
+
+import httpx
+
+from ..db.models import HNItem
+
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
+BASE_URL = "https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0"
+
+# How many stories to fetch per category
+DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT = 60
+
+
+class HNClient:
+    """Lightweight async wrapper around the HN Firebase API."""
+
+    def __init__(self, client: httpx.AsyncClient | None = None) -> None:
+        self._client = client or httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30)
+        self._owns_client = client is None
+
+    async def _get_json(self, path: str) -> Any:
+        url = f"{BASE_URL}{path}"
+        resp = await self._client.get(url)
+        resp.raise_for_status()
+        return resp.json()
+
+    async def get_top_story_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
+        ids = await self._get_json("/topstories.json")
+        return ids[:limit]
+
+    async def get_new_story_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
+        ids = await self._get_json("/newstories.json")
+        return ids[:limit]
+
+    async def get_best_story_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
+        ids = await self._get_json("/beststories.json")
+        return ids[:limit]
+
+    async def get_show_hn_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
+        ids = await self._get_json("/showstories.json")
+        return ids[:limit]
+
+    async def get_item(self, item_id: int) -> HNItem | None:
+        """Fetch a single HN item by ID."""
+        resp = await self._client.get(f"{BASE_URL}/item/{item_id}.json")
+        resp.raise_for_status()
+        # The HN API returns literal "null" for deleted/missing items
+        if resp.text.strip() in ("", "null"):
+            return None
+        data = resp.json()
+        if data is None:
+            return None
+        return HNItem(
+            id=data.get("id", 0),
+            type=data.get("type", ""),
+            title=data.get("title", ""),
+            text=data.get("text", ""),
+            url=data.get("url", ""),
+            by=data.get("by", ""),
+            score=data.get("score", 0),
+            descendants=data.get("descendants", 0),
+            time=data.get("time", 0),
+        )
+
+    async def get_items(self, item_ids: list[int]) -> list[HNItem]:
+        """Fetch multiple items concurrently."""
+        items: list[HNItem] = []
+        # Fetch in batches to avoid overwhelming the API
+        batch_size = 20
+        for i in range(0, len(item_ids), batch_size):
+            batch = item_ids[i : i + batch_size]
+            import asyncio
+
+            results = await asyncio.gather(
+                *(self.get_item(iid) for iid in batch),
+                return_exceptions=True,
+            )
+            for result in results:
+                if isinstance(result, HNItem):
+                    items.append(result)
+                elif isinstance(result, Exception):
+                    logger.warning("Failed to fetch HN item: %s", result)
+        return items
+
+    async def close(self) -> None:
+        if self._owns_client:
+            await self._client.aclose()

+"""HackerNews monitor — fetches stories, extracts companies, stores signals."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import logging
+from dataclasses import dataclass, field
+
+from ..db.models import Company, Signal, SignalType, Source
+from ..db.store import Store
+from ..extraction.company_extractor import ExtractionResult, extract_from_hn_item
+from .client import HNClient, HNItem
+
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
+
+@dataclass
+class MonitorResult:
+    """Summary of a single monitoring run."""
+
+    stories_fetched: int = 0
+    signals_found: int = 0
+    new_signals_stored: int = 0
+    new_companies: int = 0
+    errors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
+
+
+class HNMonitor:
+    """Orchestrates fetching, extraction, and storage for HackerNews."""
+
+    def __init__(self, client: HNClient, store: Store) -> None:
+        self.client = client
+        self.store = store
+
+    async def run(self, fetch_limit: int = 60) -> MonitorResult:
+        """Execute one monitoring cycle.
+
+        Pulls stories from multiple HN feeds, deduplicates, extracts
+        company signals, and persists them.
+        """
+        result = MonitorResult()
+
+        # Gather story IDs from multiple feeds and deduplicate
+        all_ids: set[int] = set()
+        try:
+            for fetch_fn in (
+                self.client.get_top_story_ids,
+                self.client.get_new_story_ids,
+                self.client.get_show_hn_ids,
+            ):
+                ids = await fetch_fn(limit=fetch_limit)
+                all_ids.update(ids)
+        except Exception as exc:
+            msg = f"Error fetching story IDs: {exc}"
+            logger.error(msg)
+            result.errors.append(msg)
+            return result
+
+        logger.info("Fetching %d unique stories from HN", len(all_ids))
+        items = await self.client.get_items(sorted(all_ids))
+        result.stories_fetched = len(items)
+
+        # Extract and store
+        for item in items:
+            extraction = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+            if extraction is None:
+                continue
+            result.signals_found += 1
+            self._store_extraction(item, extraction, result)
+
+        logger.info(
+            "Monitor run complete: %d stories, %d signals (%d new), %d new companies",
+            result.stories_fetched,
+            result.signals_found,
+            result.new_signals_stored,
+            result.new_companies,
+        )
+        return result
+
+    def _store_extraction(
+        self,
+        item: HNItem,
+        extraction: ExtractionResult,
+        result: MonitorResult,
+    ) -> None:
+        """Persist a single extraction (company + signal)."""
+        # Upsert company
+        company = Company(
+            name=extraction.company_name,
+            description=extraction.description,
+            url=item.url,
+        )
+        existing = self.store.get_company(company.name)
+        self.store.upsert_company(company)
+        if existing is None:
+            result.new_companies += 1
+
+        # Build HN URL
+        hn_url = f"https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id={item.id}"
+
+        signal = Signal(
+            company_name=extraction.company_name,
+            signal_type=extraction.signal_type,
+            source=Source.HACKERNEWS,
+            source_id=str(item.id),
+            source_url=hn_url,
+            title=item.title,
+            content=extraction.description,
+            score=item.score,
+            comments_count=item.descendants,
+        )
+
+        stored = self.store.insert_signal(signal)
+        if stored is not None:
+            result.new_signals_stored += 1

   211→+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS companies (
   212→+    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   213→+    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   214→+    description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
   215→+    url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
   216→+    first_seen_at TEXT NOT NULL
   217→+);
   218→+

   223→+    source TEXT NOT NULL,

   228→+    score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   229→+    comments_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   230→+    discovered_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   231→+    UNIQUE(source, source_id, company_name)
   232→+);

   235→+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_source ON signals(source);
   236→+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON signals(signal_type);
   237→+"""

   240→+class Store:
   241→+    """Thin wrapper around SQLite for persisting companies and signals."""
   242→+
   243→+    def __init__(self, db_path: Path = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
   244→+        self.db_path = db_path
   245→+        self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   246→+        self._conn = sqlite3.connect(str(self.db_path))
   247→+        self._conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row

   254→+        cur = self._conn.execute(

   256→+               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
   257→+               ON CONFLICT(name) DO UPDATE SET

   259→+                   url = CASE WHEN excluded.url != '' THEN excluded.url ELSE companies.url END

   273→+        row = self._conn.execute(

   275→+        ).fetchone()
   276→+        if row is None:
   277→+            return None
   278→+        return Company(**dict(row))
   279→+
   280→+    def list_companies(self, limit: int = 100) -> list[Company]:
   281→+        rows = self._conn.execute(
   282→+            "SELECT * FROM companies ORDER BY first_seen_at DESC LIMIT ?", (limit,)
   283→+        ).fetchall()
   284→+        return [Company(**dict(r)) for r in rows]
   285→+
   286→+    # -- Signals ------------------------------------------------------------
   287→+
   288→+    def insert_signal(self, signal: Signal) -> Signal | None:
   289→+        """Insert a signal. Returns None if duplicate (source+source_id+company)."""
   290→+        try:
   291→+            cur = self._conn.execute(
   292→+                """INSERT INTO signals
   293→+                   (company_name, signal_type, source, source_id, source_url,
   294→+                    title, content, score, comments_count, discovered_at)
   295→+                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
   296→+                (

   298→+                    signal.signal_type,
   299→+                    signal.source,

   301→+                    signal.source_url,

   309→+            self._conn.commit()
   310→+            signal.id = cur.lastrowid
   311→+            return signal

   315→+    def list_signals(

   318→+        source: str | None = None,

   321→+        query = "SELECT * FROM signals WHERE 1=1"

   325→+            params.append(company_name)

   328→+            params.append(source)
   329→+        query += " ORDER BY discovered_at DESC LIMIT ?"
   330→+        params.append(limit)
   331→+        rows = self._conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()
   332→+        return [Signal(**dict(r)) for r in rows]

   345→+"""Extract company mentions and classify signals from HN posts.
   346→+
   347→+Strategy:
   348→+  1. Pattern-match on well-known HN title formats (Show HN, Launch HN, etc.)
   349→+  2. Look for funding/launch keywords in title and body text
   350→+  3. Extract company name from the title using heuristics
   351→+  4. Classify the signal type (show_hn, funding, product_launch, etc.)
   352→+"""
   353→+
   354→+from __future__ import annotations
   355→+
   356→+import re
   357→+from dataclasses import dataclass
   358→+
   359→+from ..db.models import HNItem, SignalType
   360→+
   361→+
   362→+@dataclass
   363→+class ExtractionResult:
   364→+    """Result of extracting a company signal from an HN item."""
   365→+
   366→+    company_name: str
   367→+    signal_type: SignalType
   368→+    description: str
   369→+
   370→+
   371→+# Patterns ordered by specificity
   372→+SHOW_HN_RE = re.compile(r"^Show HN:\s*(.+)", re.IGNORECASE)
   373→+LAUNCH_HN_RE = re.compile(r"^Launch HN:\s*(.+)", re.IGNORECASE)
   374→+
   375→+# Funding patterns: "Company raises $X", "Company announces Series A", etc.
   376→+FUNDING_RE = re.compile(
   377→+    r"(.+?)\s+(?:raises?|raised|secures?|secured|closes?|closed|announces?|announced)"
   378→+    r"\s+(?:\$[\d.]+[BMK]?\s+)?(?:(?:seed|series\s+[a-z]|funding|round|investment))",
   379→+    re.IGNORECASE,
   380→+)
   381→+
   382→+# "Company launches X", "We built X", "Introducing X"
   383→+LAUNCH_KEYWORDS_RE = re.compile(
   384→+    r"(.+?)\s+(?:launch(?:es|ed)?|releas(?:es|ed|ing)|introducing|we\s+built|just\s+shipped)",
   385→+    re.IGNORECASE,
   386→+)
   387→+
   388→+# YC batch pattern: "Company (YC S24)"
   389→+YC_BATCH_RE = re.compile(r"(.+?)\s*\(YC\s+[A-Z]\d{2}\)", re.IGNORECASE)
   390→+
   391→+# Common title fluff to strip when extracting company names
   392→+FLUFF_RE = re.compile(
   393→+    r"\s*[-–—:|]\s*(an?\s+|the\s+)?(open[- ]source\s+)?.*$",
   394→+    re.IGNORECASE,
   395→+)
   396→+
   397→+# Words that are almost certainly not company names
   398→+STOP_WORDS = frozenset(
   399→+    {
   400→+        "how",
   401→+        "why",
   402→+        "what",
   403→+        "when",
   404→+        "where",
   405→+        "who",
   406→+        "ask",
   407→+        "tell",
   408→+        "the",
   409→+        "a",
   410→+        "an",
   411→+        "is",
   412→+        "are",
   413→+        "was",
   414→+        "i",
   415→+        "we",
   416→+        "my",
   417→+        "our",
   418→+    }
   419→+)
   420→+
   421→+
   422→+def _clean_company_name(raw: str) -> str:
   423→+    """Best-effort cleanup of a raw company name string."""
   424→+    # Remove markdown links
   425→+    name = re.sub(r"\[([^\]]+)\]\([^)]+\)", r"\1", raw)
   426→+    # Remove HTML tags
   427→+    name = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", name)
   428→+    # Take text before common separators that introduce a description
   429→+    name = FLUFF_RE.sub("", name)
   430→+    name = name.strip().strip(".,;:!?\"'")
   431→+
   432→+    # If what's left is very long, take the first segment before a dash/colon
   433→+    if len(name) > 60:
   434→+        parts = re.split(r"\s*[-–—:]\s*", name, maxsplit=1)
   435→+        name = parts[0].strip()
   436→+
   437→+    return name
   438→+
   439→+
   440→+def _looks_like_company(name: str) -> bool:
   441→+    """Quick heuristic to filter out obvious non-company extractions."""
   442→+    if not name or len(name) < 2:
   443→+        return False
   444→+    first_word = name.split()[0].lower()
   445→+    if first_word in STOP_WORDS:
   446→+        return False
   447→+    # Reject if it's all lowercase and reads like a sentence
   448→+    if len(name.split()) > 6:
   449→+        return False
   450→+    return True
   451→+
   452→+
   453→+def extract_from_hn_item(item: HNItem) -> ExtractionResult | None:
   454→+    """Try to extract a company signal from an HN item.
   455→+
   456→+    Returns None if no company signal is detected.
   457→+    """
   458→+    title = item.title.strip()
   459→+    if not title:
   460→+        return None
   461→+
   462→+    # --- Show HN ---
   463→+    m = SHOW_HN_RE.match(title)
   464→+    if m:
   465→+        raw = m.group(1)
   466→+        name = _clean_company_name(raw)
   467→+        if _looks_like_company(name):
   468→+            return ExtractionResult(
   469→+                company_name=name,
   470→+                signal_type=SignalType.SHOW_HN,
   471→+                description=raw.strip(),
   472→+            )
   473→+
   474→+    # --- Launch HN ---
   475→+    m = LAUNCH_HN_RE.match(title)
   476→+    if m:
   477→+        raw = m.group(1)
   478→+        name = _clean_company_name(raw)
   479→+        if _looks_like_company(name):
   480→+            return ExtractionResult(
   481→+                company_name=name,
   482→+                signal_type=SignalType.PRODUCT_LAUNCH,
   483→+                description=raw.strip(),
   484→+            )
   485→+
   486→+    # --- YC batch mentions ---
   487→+    m = YC_BATCH_RE.match(title)
   488→+    if m:
   489→+        name = _clean_company_name(m.group(1))
   490→+        if _looks_like_company(name):
   491→+            return ExtractionResult(
   492→+                company_name=name,
   493→+                signal_type=SignalType.FUNDING,
   494→+                description=title,
   495→+            )
   496→+
   497→+    # --- Funding announcements ---
   498→+    m = FUNDING_RE.match(title)
   499→+    if m:
   500→+        name = _clean_company_name(m.group(1))
   501→+        if _looks_like_company(name):
   502→+            return ExtractionResult(
   503→+                company_name=name,
   504→+                signal_type=SignalType.FUNDING,
   505→+                description=title,
   506→+            )
   507→+
   508→+    # --- Product launches / "we built" ---
   509→+    m = LAUNCH_KEYWORDS_RE.match(title)
   510→+    if m:
   511→+        name = _clean_company_name(m.group(1))
   512→+        if _looks_like_company(name):
   513→+            return ExtractionResult(
   514→+                company_name=name,
   515→+                signal_type=SignalType.PRODUCT_LAUNCH,
   516→+                description=title,
   517→+            )
   518→+
   519→+    return None

   529→+"""Async client for the HackerNews Firebase API.
   530→+
   531→+Docs: https://github.com/HackerNews/API
   532→+Base URL: https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0
   533→+"""
   534→+
   535→+from __future__ import annotations
   536→+
   537→+import logging
   538→+from typing import Any
   539→+
   540→+import httpx
   541→+
   542→+from ..db.models import HNItem
   543→+
   544→+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
   545→+
   546→+BASE_URL = "https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0"
   547→+
   548→+# How many stories to fetch per category
   549→+DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT = 60
   550→+
   551→+
   552→+class HNClient:
   553→+    """Lightweight async wrapper around the HN Firebase API."""
   554→+
   555→+    def __init__(self, client: httpx.AsyncClient | None = None) -> None:
   556→+        self._client = client or httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30)
   557→+        self._owns_client = client is None
   558→+
   559→+    async def _get_json(self, path: str) -> Any:
   560→+        url = f"{BASE_URL}{path}"
   561→+        resp = await self._client.get(url)
   562→+        resp.raise_for_status()
   563→+        return resp.json()
   564→+
   565→+    async def get_top_story_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
   566→+        ids = await self._get_json("/topstories.json")
   567→+        return ids[:limit]
   568→+
   569→+    async def get_new_story_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
   570→+        ids = await self._get_json("/newstories.json")
   571→+        return ids[:limit]
   572→+
   573→+    async def get_best_story_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
   574→+        ids = await self._get_json("/beststories.json")
   575→+        return ids[:limit]
   576→+
   577→+    async def get_show_hn_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
   578→+        ids = await self._get_json("/showstories.json")
   579→+        return ids[:limit]
   580→+
   581→+    async def get_item(self, item_id: int) -> HNItem | None:
   582→+        """Fetch a single HN item by ID."""
   583→+        resp = await self._client.get(f"{BASE_URL}/item/{item_id}.json")
   584→+        resp.raise_for_status()
   585→+        # The HN API returns literal "null" for deleted/missing items
   586→+        if resp.text.strip() in ("", "null"):
   587→+            return None
   588→+        data = resp.json()
   589→+        if data is None:
   590→+            return None
   591→+        return HNItem(
   592→+            id=data.get("id", 0),
   593→+            type=data.get("type", ""),
   594→+            title=data.get("title", ""),
   595→+            text=data.get("text", ""),
   596→+            url=data.get("url", ""),
   597→+            by=data.get("by", ""),
   598→+            score=data.get("score", 0),
   599→+            descendants=data.get("descendants", 0),
   600→+            time=data.get("time", 0),
   601→+        )
   602→+
   603→+    async def get_items(self, item_ids: list[int]) -> list[HNItem]:
   604→+        """Fetch multiple items concurrently."""
   605→+        items: list[HNItem] = []
   606→+        # Fetch in batches to avoid overwhelming the API
   607→+        batch_size = 20
   608→+        for i in range(0, len(item_ids), batch_size):
   609→+            batch = item_ids[i : i + batch_size]
   610→+            import asyncio
   611→+
   612→+            results = await asyncio.gather(
   613→+                *(self.get_item(iid) for iid in batch),
   614→+                return_exceptions=True,
   615→+            )
   616→+            for result in results:
   617→+                if isinstance(result, HNItem):
   618→+                    items.append(result)
   619→+                elif isinstance(result, Exception):
   620→+                    logger.warning("Failed to fetch HN item: %s", result)
   621→+        return items
   622→+
   623→+    async def close(self) -> None:
   624→+        if self._owns_client:
   625→+            await self._client.aclose()

   632→+"""HackerNews monitor — fetches stories, extracts companies, stores signals."""
   633→+
   634→+from __future__ import annotations
   635→+
   636→+import logging
   637→+from dataclasses import dataclass, field
   638→+
   639→+from ..db.models import Company, Signal, SignalType, Source
   640→+from ..db.store import Store
   641→+from ..extraction.company_extractor import ExtractionResult, extract_from_hn_item
   642→+from .client import HNClient, HNItem
   643→+
   644→+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
   645→+
   646→+
   647→+@dataclass
   648→+class MonitorResult:
   649→+    """Summary of a single monitoring run."""
   650→+
   651→+    stories_fetched: int = 0
   652→+    signals_found: int = 0
   653→+    new_signals_stored: int = 0
   654→+    new_companies: int = 0
   655→+    errors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
   656→+
   657→+
   658→+class HNMonitor:
   659→+    """Orchestrates fetching, extraction, and storage for HackerNews."""
   660→+
   661→+    def __init__(self, client: HNClient, store: Store) -> None:
   662→+        self.client = client
   663→+        self.store = store
   664→+
   665→+    async def run(self, fetch_limit: int = 60) -> MonitorResult:
   666→+        """Execute one monitoring cycle.
   667→+
   668→+        Pulls stories from multiple HN feeds, deduplicates, extracts
   669→+        company signals, and persists them.
   670→+        """
   671→+        result = MonitorResult()
   672→+
   673→+        # Gather story IDs from multiple feeds and deduplicate
   674→+        all_ids: set[int] = set()
   675→+        try:
   676→+            for fetch_fn in (
   677→+                self.client.get_top_story_ids,
   678→+                self.client.get_new_story_ids,
   679→+                self.client.get_show_hn_ids,
   680→+            ):
   681→+                ids = await fetch_fn(limit=fetch_limit)
   682→+                all_ids.update(ids)
   683→+        except Exception as exc:
   684→+            msg = f"Error fetching story IDs: {exc}"
   685→+            logger.error(msg)
   686→+            result.errors.append(msg)
   687→+            return result
   688→+
   689→+        logger.info("Fetching %d unique stories from HN", len(all_ids))
   690→+        items = await self.client.get_items(sorted(all_ids))
   691→+        result.stories_fetched = len(items)
   692→+
   693→+        # Extract and store
   694→+        for item in items:
   695→+            extraction = extract_from_hn_item(item)
   696→+            if extraction is None:
   697→+                continue
   698→+            result.signals_found += 1
   699→+            self._store_extraction(item, extraction, result)
   700→+
   701→+        logger.info(
   702→+            "Monitor run complete: %d stories, %d signals (%d new), %d new companies",
   703→+            result.stories_fetched,
   704→+            result.signals_found,
   705→+            result.new_signals_stored,
   706→+            result.new_companies,
   707→+        )
   708→+        return result
   709→+
   710→+    def _store_extraction(
   711→+        self,
   712→+        item: HNItem,
   713→+        extraction: ExtractionResult,
   714→+        result: MonitorResult,
   715→+    ) -> None:
   716→+        """Persist a single extraction (company + signal)."""
   717→+        # Upsert company
   718→+        company = Company(
   719→+            name=extraction.company_name,
   720→+            description=extraction.description,
   721→+            url=item.url,
   722→+        )
   723→+        existing = self.store.get_company(company.name)
   724→+        self.store.upsert_company(company)
   725→+        if existing is None:
   726→+            result.new_companies += 1
   727→+
   728→+        # Build HN URL
   729→+        hn_url = f"https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id={item.id}"
   730→+
   731→+        signal = Signal(
   732→+            company_name=extraction.company_name,
   733→+            signal_type=extraction.signal_type,
   734→+            source=Source.HACKERNEWS,
   735→+            source_id=str(item.id),
   736→+            source_url=hn_url,
   737→+            title=item.title,
   738→+            content=extraction.description,
   739→+            score=item.score,
   740→+            comments_count=item.descendants,
   741→+        )
   742→+
   743→+        stored = self.store.insert_signal(signal)
   744→+        if stored is not None:
   745→+            result.new_signals_stored += 1

+"""Simple CLI entry-point for running the HN monitor."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import asyncio
+import logging
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from .db.store import Store
+from .hn.client import HNClient
+from .hn.monitor import HNMonitor
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    logging.basicConfig(
+        level=logging.INFO,
+        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s: %(message)s",
+    )
+    logger = logging.getLogger("company_sourcing")
+
+    db_path = Path("data/sourcing.db")
+    fetch_limit = 60
+
+    # Simple arg handling
+    for arg in sys.argv[1:]:
+        if arg.startswith("--db="):
+            db_path = Path(arg.split("=", 1)[1])
+        elif arg.startswith("--limit="):
+            fetch_limit = int(arg.split("=", 1)[1])
+
+    logger.info("Starting HN monitor (db=%s, limit=%d)", db_path, fetch_limit)
+    store = Store(db_path)
+    client = HNClient()
+
+    async def _run() -> None:
+        monitor = HNMonitor(client=client, store=store)
+        result = await monitor.run(fetch_limit=fetch_limit)
+        print(f"\nDone! Fetched {result.stories_fetched} stories.")
+        print(f"Signals found: {result.signals_found}")
+        print(f"New signals stored: {result.new_signals_stored}")
+        print(f"New companies discovered: {result.new_companies}")
+        if result.errors:
+            print(f"Errors: {len(result.errors)}")
+            for err in result.errors:
+                print(f"  - {err}")
+        await client.close()
+        store.close()
+
+    asyncio.run(_run())
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+"""Database models using Pydantic for validation and SQLite for storage."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import enum
+from datetime import datetime, timezone
+
+from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
+
+
+class Source(str, enum.Enum):
+    """Where we found the signal."""
+
+    HACKERNEWS = "hackernews"
+    TWITTER = "twitter"
+    GOOGLE_NEWS = "google_news"
+
+
+class SignalType(str, enum.Enum):
+    """What kind of signal this is."""
+
+    SHOW_HN = "show_hn"
+    PRODUCT_LAUNCH = "product_launch"
+    FUNDING = "funding"
+    HIRING = "hiring"
+    GENERAL_MENTION = "general_mention"
+
+
+class Company(BaseModel):
+    """An extracted company entity."""
+
+    id: int | None = None
+    name: str
+    description: str = ""
+    url: str = ""
+    first_seen_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
+
+
+class Signal(BaseModel):
+    """A signal about a company from a source."""
+
+    id: int | None = None
+    company_name: str
+    signal_type: SignalType
+    source: Source
+    source_id: str  # e.g. HN story ID
+    source_url: str = ""
+    title: str = ""
+    content: str = ""
+    score: int = 0  # HN points, tweet likes, etc.
+    comments_count: int = 0
+    discovered_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
+
+
+class HNItem(BaseModel):
+    """A raw HackerNews item from the API."""
+
+    id: int
+    type: str = ""
+    title: str = ""
+    text: str = ""
+    url: str = ""
+    by: str = ""
+    score: int = 0
+    descendants: int = 0  # comment count
+    time: int = 0

+"""SQLite storage layer for companies and signals."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import sqlite3
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from .models import Company, Signal
+
+DEFAULT_DB_PATH = Path("data/sourcing.db")
+
+SCHEMA = """\
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS companies (
+    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
+    name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
+    description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    first_seen_at TEXT NOT NULL
+);
+
+CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS signals (
+    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
+    company_name TEXT NOT NULL,
+    signal_type TEXT NOT NULL,
+    source TEXT NOT NULL,
+    source_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    source_url TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    title TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    content TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
+    score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
+    comments_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
+    discovered_at TEXT NOT NULL,
+    UNIQUE(source, source_id, company_name)
+);
+
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_company ON signals(company_name);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_source ON signals(source);
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON signals(signal_type);
+"""
+
+
+class Store:
+    """Thin wrapper around SQLite for persisting companies and signals."""
+
+    def __init__(self, db_path: Path = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
+        self.db_path = db_path
+        self.db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        self._conn = sqlite3.connect(str(self.db_path))
+        self._conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
+        self._conn.executescript(SCHEMA)
+
+    # -- Companies ----------------------------------------------------------
+
+    def upsert_company(self, company: Company) -> Company:
+        """Insert or ignore a company (keyed by name)."""
+        cur = self._conn.execute(
+            """INSERT INTO companies (name, description, url, first_seen_at)
+               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
+               ON CONFLICT(name) DO UPDATE SET
+                   description = CASE WHEN excluded.description != '' THEN excluded.description ELSE companies.description END,
+                   url = CASE WHEN excluded.url != '' THEN excluded.url ELSE companies.url END
+            """,
+            (
+                company.name,
+                company.description,
+                company.url,
+                company.first_seen_at.isoformat(),
+            ),
+        )
+        self._conn.commit()
+        company.id = cur.lastrowid
+        return company
+
+    def get_company(self, name: str) -> Company | None:
+        row = self._conn.execute(
+            "SELECT * FROM companies WHERE name = ?", (name,)
+        ).fetchone()
+        if row is None:
+            return None
+        return Company(**dict(row))
+
+    def list_companies(self, limit: int = 100) -> list[Company]:
+        rows = self._conn.execute(
+            "SELECT * FROM companies ORDER BY first_seen_at DESC LIMIT ?", (limit,)
+        ).fetchall()
+        return [Company(**dict(r)) for r in rows]
+
+    # -- Signals ------------------------------------------------------------
+
+    def insert_signal(self, signal: Signal) -> Signal | None:
+        """Insert a signal. Returns None if duplicate (source+source_id+company)."""
+        try:
+            cur = self._conn.execute(
+                """INSERT INTO signals
+                   (company_name, signal_type, source, source_id, source_url,
+                    title, content, score, comments_count, discovered_at)
+                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
+                (
+                    signal.company_name,
+                    signal.signal_type,
+                    signal.source,
+                    signal.source_id,
+                    signal.source_url,
+                    signal.title,
+                    signal.content,
+                    signal.score,
+                    signal.comments_count,
+                    signal.discovered_at.isoformat(),
+                ),
+            )
+            self._conn.commit()
+            signal.id = cur.lastrowid
+            return signal
+        except sqlite3.IntegrityError:
+            return None
+
+    def list_signals(
+        self,
+        company_name: str | None = None,
+        source: str | None = None,
+        limit: int = 100,
+    ) -> list[Signal]:
+        query = "SELECT * FROM signals WHERE 1=1"
+        params: list[object] = []
+        if company_name:
+            query += " AND company_name = ?"
+            params.append(company_name)
+        if source:
+            query += " AND source = ?"
+            params.append(source)
+        query += " ORDER BY discovered_at DESC LIMIT ?"
+        params.append(limit)
+        rows = self._conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()
+        return [Signal(**dict(r)) for r in rows]
+
+    def close(self) -> None:
+        self._conn.close()

@@ -0,0 +1,175 @@
+"""Extract company mentions and classify signals from HN posts.
+
+Strategy:
+  1. Pattern-match on well-known HN title formats (Show HN, Launch HN, etc.)
+  2. Look for funding/launch keywords in title and body text
+  3. Extract company name from the title using heuristics
+  4. Classify the signal type (show_hn, funding, product_launch, etc.)
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import re
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+
+from ..db.models import HNItem, SignalType
+
+
+@dataclass
+class ExtractionResult:
+    """Result of extracting a company signal from an HN item."""
+
+    company_name: str
+    signal_type: SignalType
+    description: str
+
+
+# Patterns ordered by specificity
+SHOW_HN_RE = re.compile(r"^Show HN:\s*(.+)", re.IGNORECASE)
+LAUNCH_HN_RE = re.compile(r"^Launch HN:\s*(.+)", re.IGNORECASE)
+
+# Funding patterns: "Company raises $X", "Company announces Series A", etc.
+FUNDING_RE = re.compile(
+    r"(.+?)\s+(?:raises?|raised|secures?|secured|closes?|closed|announces?|announced)"
+    r"\s+(?:\$[\d.]+[BMK]?\s+)?(?:(?:seed|series\s+[a-z]|funding|round|investment))",
+    re.IGNORECASE,
+)
+
+# "Company launches X", "We built X", "Introducing X"
+LAUNCH_KEYWORDS_RE = re.compile(
+    r"(.+?)\s+(?:launch(?:es|ed)?|releas(?:es|ed|ing)|introducing|we\s+built|just\s+shipped)",
+    re.IGNORECASE,
+)
+
+# YC batch pattern: "Company (YC S24)"
+YC_BATCH_RE = re.compile(r"(.+?)\s*\(YC\s+[A-Z]\d{2}\)", re.IGNORECASE)
+
+# Common title fluff to strip when extracting company names
+FLUFF_RE = re.compile(
+    r"\s*[-–—:|]\s*(an?\s+|the\s+)?(open[- ]source\s+)?.*$",
+    re.IGNORECASE,
+)
+
+# Words that are almost certainly not company names
+STOP_WORDS = frozenset(
+    {
+        "how",
+        "why",
+        "what",
+        "when",
+        "where",
+        "who",
+        "ask",
+        "tell",
+        "the",
+        "a",
+        "an",
+        "is",
+        "are",
+        "was",
+        "i",
+        "we",
+        "my",
+        "our",
+    }
+)
+
+
+def _clean_company_name(raw: str) -> str:
+    """Best-effort cleanup of a raw company name string."""
+    # Remove markdown links
+    name = re.sub(r"\[([^\]]+)\]\([^)]+\)", r"\1", raw)
+    # Remove HTML tags
+    name = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "", name)
+    # Take text before common separators that introduce a description
+    name = FLUFF_RE.sub("", name)
+    name = name.strip().strip(".,;:!?\"'")
+
+    # If what's left is very long, take the first segment before a dash/colon
+    if len(name) > 60:
+        parts = re.split(r"\s*[-–—:]\s*", name, maxsplit=1)
+        name = parts[0].strip()
+
+    return name
+
+
+def _looks_like_company(name: str) -> bool:
+    """Quick heuristic to filter out obvious non-company extractions."""
+    if not name or len(name) < 2:
+        return False
+    first_word = name.split()[0].lower()
+    if first_word in STOP_WORDS:
+        return False
+    # Reject if it's all lowercase and reads like a sentence
+    if len(name.split()) > 6:
+        return False
+    return True
+
+
+def extract_from_hn_item(item: HNItem) -> ExtractionResult | None:
+    """Try to extract a company signal from an HN item.
+
+    Returns None if no company signal is detected.
+    """
+    title = item.title.strip()
+    if not title:
+        return None
+
+    # --- Show HN ---
+    m = SHOW_HN_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        raw = m.group(1)
+        name = _clean_company_name(raw)
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.SHOW_HN,
+                description=raw.strip(),
+            )
+
+    # --- Launch HN ---
+    m = LAUNCH_HN_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        raw = m.group(1)
+        name = _clean_company_name(raw)
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.PRODUCT_LAUNCH,
+                description=raw.strip(),
+            )
+
+    # --- YC batch mentions ---
+    m = YC_BATCH_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        name = _clean_company_name(m.group(1))
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.FUNDING,
+                description=title,
+            )
+
+    # --- Funding announcements ---
+    m = FUNDING_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        name = _clean_company_name(m.group(1))
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.FUNDING,
+                description=title,
+            )
+
+    # --- Product launches / "we built" ---
+    m = LAUNCH_KEYWORDS_RE.match(title)
+    if m:
+        name = _clean_company_name(m.group(1))
+        if _looks_like_company(name):
+            return ExtractionResult(
+                company_name=name,
+                signal_type=SignalType.PRODUCT_LAUNCH,
+                description=title,
+            )
+
+    return None

+"""Async client for the HackerNews Firebase API.
+
+Docs: https://github.com/HackerNews/API
+Base URL: https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import logging
+from typing import Any
+
+import httpx
+
+from ..db.models import HNItem
+
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
+BASE_URL = "https://hacker-news.firebaseio.com/v0"
+
+# How many stories to fetch per category
+DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT = 60
+
+
+class HNClient:
+    """Lightweight async wrapper around the HN Firebase API."""
+
+    def __init__(self, client: httpx.AsyncClient | None = None) -> None:
+        self._client = client or httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30)
+        self._owns_client = client is None
+
+    async def _get_json(self, path: str) -> Any:
+        url = f"{BASE_URL}{path}"
+        resp = await self._client.get(url)
+        resp.raise_for_status()
+        return resp.json()
+
+    async def get_top_story_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
+        ids = await self._get_json("/topstories.json")
+        return ids[:limit]
+
+    async def get_new_story_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
+        ids = await self._get_json("/newstories.json")
+        return ids[:limit]
+
+    async def get_best_story_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
+        ids = await self._get_json("/beststories.json")
+        return ids[:limit]
+
+    async def get_show_hn_ids(self, limit: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_LIMIT) -> list[int]:
+        ids = await self._get_json("/showstories.json")
+        return ids[:limit]
+
+    async def get_item(self, item_id: int) -> HNItem | None:
+        """Fetch a single HN item by ID."""
+        resp = await self._client.get(f"{BASE_URL}/item/{item_id}.json")
+        resp.raise_for_status()
+        # The HN API returns literal "null" for deleted/missing items
+        if resp.text.strip() in ("", "null"):
+            return None
+        data = resp.json()
+        if data is None:
+            return None
+        return HNItem(
+            id=data.get("id", 0),
+            type=data.get("type", ""),
+            title=data.get("title", ""),
+            text=data.get("text", ""),
+            url=data.get("url", ""),
+            by=data.get("by", ""),
+            score=data.get("score", 0),
+            descendants=data.get("descendants", 0),
+            time=data.get("time", 0),
+        )
+
+    async def get_items(self, item_ids: list[int]) -> list[HNItem]:
+        """Fetch multiple items concurrently."""
+        items: list[HNItem] = []
+        # Fetch in batches to avoid overwhelming the API
+        batch_size = 20
+        for i in range(0, len(item_ids), batch_size):
+            batch = item_ids[i : i + batch_size]
+            import asyncio
+
+            results = await asyncio.gather(
+                *(self.get_item(iid) for iid in batch),
+                return_exceptions=True,
+            )
+            for result in results:
+                if isinstance(result, HNItem):
+                    items.append(result)
+                elif isinstance(result, Exception):
+                    logger.warning("Failed to fetch HN item: %s", result)
+        return items
+
+    async def close(self) -> None:
+        if self._owns_client:
+            await self._client.aclose()

+"""HackerNews monitor — fetches stories, extracts companies, stores signals."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import logging
+from dataclasses import dataclass, field
+
+from ..db.models import Company, Signal, SignalType, Source
+from ..db.store import Store
+from ..extraction.company_extractor import ExtractionResult, extract_from_hn_item
+from .client import HNClient, HNItem
+
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
+
+@dataclass
+class MonitorResult:
+    """Summary of a single monitoring run."""
+
+    stories_fetched: int = 0
+    signals_found: int = 0
+    new_signals_stored: int = 0
+    new_companies: int = 0
+    errors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
+
+
+class HNMonitor:
+    """Orchestrates fetching, extraction, and storage for HackerNews."""
+
+    def __init__(self, client: HNClient, store: Store) -> None:
+        self.client = client
+        self.store = store
+
+    async def run(self, fetch_limit: int = 60) -> MonitorResult:
+        """Execute one monitoring cycle.
+
+        Pulls stories from multiple HN feeds, deduplicates, extracts
+        company signals, and persists them.
+        """
+        result = MonitorResult()
+
+        # Gather story IDs from multiple feeds and deduplicate
+        all_ids: set[int] = set()
+        try:
+            for fetch_fn in (
+                self.client.get_top_story_ids,
+                self.client.get_new_story_ids,
+                self.client.get_show_hn_ids,
+            ):
+                ids = await fetch_fn(limit=fetch_limit)
+                all_ids.update(ids)
+        except Exception as exc:
+            msg = f"Error fetching story IDs: {exc}"
+            logger.error(msg)
+            result.errors.append(msg)
+            return result
+
+        logger.info("Fetching %d unique stories from HN", len(all_ids))
+        items = await self.client.get_items(sorted(all_ids))
+        result.stories_fetched = len(items)
+
+        # Extract and store
+        for item in items:
+            extraction = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+            if extraction is None:
+                continue
+            result.signals_found += 1
+            self._store_extraction(item, extraction, result)
+
+        logger.info(
+            "Monitor run complete: %d stories, %d signals (%d new), %d new companies",
+            result.stories_fetched,
+            result.signals_found,
+            result.new_signals_stored,
+            result.new_companies,
+        )
+        return result
+
+    def _store_extraction(
+        self,
+        item: HNItem,
+        extraction: ExtractionResult,
+        result: MonitorResult,
+    ) -> None:
+        """Persist a single extraction (company + signal)."""
+        # Upsert company
+        company = Company(
+            name=extraction.company_name,
+            description=extraction.description,
+            url=item.url,
+        )
+        existing = self.store.get_company(company.name)
+        self.store.upsert_company(company)
+        if existing is None:
+            result.new_companies += 1
+
+        # Build HN URL
+        hn_url = f"https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id={item.id}"
+
+        signal = Signal(
+            company_name=extraction.company_name,
+            signal_type=extraction.signal_type,
+            source=Source.HACKERNEWS,
+            source_id=str(item.id),
+            source_url=hn_url,
+            title=item.title,
+            content=extraction.description,
+            score=item.score,
+            comments_count=item.descendants,
+        )
+
+        stored = self.store.insert_signal(signal)
+        if stored is not None:
+            result.new_signals_stored += 1

+"""Tests for company extraction from HN items."""
+
+from company_sourcing.db.models import HNItem, SignalType
+from company_sourcing.extraction.company_extractor import (
+    ExtractionResult,
+    _clean_company_name,
+    _looks_like_company,
+    extract_from_hn_item,
+)
+
+
+def _make_item(**kwargs) -> HNItem:
+    defaults = dict(id=1, type="story", title="", score=10, descendants=5)
+    defaults.update(kwargs)
+    return HNItem(**defaults)
+
+
+class TestCleanCompanyName:
+    def test_strips_description_after_dash(self):
+        assert _clean_company_name("Acme – the best tool") == "Acme"
+
+    def test_strips_description_after_colon(self):
+        assert _clean_company_name("Acme: an open-source widget") == "Acme"
+
+    def test_removes_html_tags(self):
+        assert _clean_company_name("<b>Acme</b>") == "Acme"
+
+    def test_removes_markdown_links(self):
+        assert _clean_company_name("[Acme](https://acme.com)") == "Acme"
+
+    def test_truncates_long_names(self):
+        long = "SuperLong Company Name That Goes On And On And Should Be Trimmed Down - extra description"
+        result = _clean_company_name(long)
+        # Should strip the "- extra description" part at minimum
+        assert "extra description" not in result
+
+
+class TestLooksLikeCompany:
+    def test_rejects_empty(self):
+        assert not _looks_like_company("")
+
+    def test_rejects_stop_word_start(self):
+        assert not _looks_like_company("How to build something")
+
+    def test_rejects_very_long_phrases(self):
+        assert not _looks_like_company("this is a very long sentence that is not a company")
+
+    def test_accepts_simple_name(self):
+        assert _looks_like_company("Acme Corp")
+
+    def test_accepts_single_word(self):
+        assert _looks_like_company("Stripe")
+
+
+class TestExtractFromHNItem:
+    def test_show_hn_extraction(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="Show HN: Acme – a tool for doing things")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is not None
+        assert result.company_name == "Acme"
+        assert result.signal_type == SignalType.SHOW_HN
+
+    def test_show_hn_with_url(self):
+        item = _make_item(
+            title="Show HN: FastDB – a fast database for analytics",
+            url="https://fastdb.io",
+        )
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is not None
+        assert result.company_name == "FastDB"
+        assert result.signal_type == SignalType.SHOW_HN
+
+    def test_launch_hn(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="Launch HN: Vercel – Frontend Cloud")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is not None
+        assert result.company_name == "Vercel"
+        assert result.signal_type == SignalType.PRODUCT_LAUNCH
+
+    def test_funding_announcement(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="Acme raises $10M Series A to reinvent widgets")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is not None
+        assert result.company_name == "Acme"
+        assert result.signal_type == SignalType.FUNDING
+
+    def test_funding_secured(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="DataCo secured $5M seed funding")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is not None
+        assert result.company_name == "DataCo"
+        assert result.signal_type == SignalType.FUNDING
+
+    def test_yc_batch_mention(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="Acme (YC S24) – AI-powered widgets")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is not None
+        assert result.company_name == "Acme"
+        assert result.signal_type == SignalType.FUNDING
+
+    def test_product_launch_keyword(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="Acme launches v2.0 of their platform")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is not None
+        assert result.company_name == "Acme"
+        assert result.signal_type == SignalType.PRODUCT_LAUNCH
+
+    def test_we_built_pattern(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="CloudTech just shipped a new API gateway")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is not None
+        assert result.company_name == "CloudTech"
+        assert result.signal_type == SignalType.PRODUCT_LAUNCH
+
+    def test_no_extraction_for_question(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="How do you manage your infrastructure?")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is None
+
+    def test_no_extraction_for_generic_news(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="The state of web development in 2026")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is None
+
+    def test_no_extraction_for_empty_title(self):
+        item = _make_item(title="")
+        result = extract_from_hn_item(item)
+        assert result is None

+"""Tests for the HN monitor integration."""
+

+
+class TestHNMonitor:
+    async def test_full_run_with_show_hn(self, mock_client, store):
+        # Simulate feeds returning overlapping IDs
+        mock_client.get_top_story_ids.return_value = [1, 2]
+        mock_client.get_new_story_ids.return_value = [2, 3]

+
+        items = [

+
+        monitor = HNMonitor(client=mock_client, store=store)
+        result = await monitor.run(fetch_limit=10)
+

+        assert result.signals_found == 2
+        assert result.new_signals_stored == 2

+        # Verify DB state

+        names = {c.name for c in companies}
+        assert "Acme" in names
+        assert "BetaCo" in names
+
+        signals = store.list_signals()
+        assert len(signals) == 2

+        mock_client.get_top_story_ids.return_value = [1]

+

+

+
+
+@pytest.fixture
+def store(tmp_path):
+    s = Store(db_path=tmp_path / "test.db")
+    yield s
+    s.close()
+
+
+class TestCompanies:
+    def test_upsert_and_get(self, store):
+        company = Company(name="Acme", description="Widgets", url="https://acme.com")
+        store.upsert_company(company)
+        fetched = store.get_company("Acme")
+        assert fetched is not None
+        assert fetched.name == "Acme"
+        assert fetched.description == "Widgets"
+
+    def test_upsert_preserves_existing_description(self, store):
+        store.upsert_company(Company(name="Acme", description="Original"))
+        store.upsert_company(Company(name="Acme", description=""))
+        fetched = store.get_company("Acme")
+        assert fetched.description == "Original"
+
+    def test_upsert_updates_nonempty_description(self, store):
+        store.upsert_company(Company(name="Acme", description="Original"))
+        store.upsert_company(Company(name="Acme", description="Updated"))
+        fetched = store.get_company("Acme")
+        assert fetched.description == "Updated"
+
+    def test_get_nonexistent(self, store):
+        assert store.get_company("NoSuch") is None
+
+    def test_list_companies(self, store):
+        store.upsert_company(Company(name="A"))
+        store.upsert_company(Company(name="B"))
+        companies = store.list_companies()
+        names = {c.name for c in companies}
+        assert names == {"A", "B"}
+
+
+class TestSignals:
+    def _make_signal(self, **kwargs):
+        defaults = dict(
+            company_name="Acme",
+            signal_type=SignalType.SHOW_HN,
+            source=Source.HACKERNEWS,
+            source_id="123",
+            source_url="https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=123",
+            title="Show HN: Acme",
+            content="A cool thing",
+            score=42,
+            comments_count=10,
+        )
+        defaults.update(kwargs)
+        return Signal(**defaults)
+
+    def test_insert_and_list(self, store):
+        signal = self._make_signal()
+        stored = store.insert_signal(signal)
+        assert stored is not None
+        assert stored.id is not None
+
+        signals = store.list_signals()
+        assert len(signals) == 1
+        assert signals[0].company_name == "Acme"
+
+    def test_duplicate_rejected(self, store):
+        signal = self._make_signal()
+        store.insert_signal(signal)
+        dup = store.insert_signal(signal)
+        assert dup is None
+        assert len(store.list_signals()) == 1
+
+    def test_filter_by_company(self, store):
+        store.insert_signal(self._make_signal(company_name="Acme", source_id="1"))
+        store.insert_signal(self._make_signal(company_name="Beta", source_id="2"))
+        signals = store.list_signals(company_name="Acme")
+        assert len(signals) == 1
+
+    def test_filter_by_source(self, store):
+        store.insert_signal(self._make_signal(source_id="1"))
+        signals = store.list_signals(source="hackernews")
+        assert len(signals) == 1
+        signals = store.list_signals(source="twitter")
+        assert len(signals) == 0
